relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul asks what advantage remains in Judean covenant identity.
The oracles of the Divine are a real entrustment.
Human untrust does not cancel Divine trustworthiness.
The Divine remains true through every human falsehood.
Human injustice cannot make Divine judgment unjust.
The Judge of the world must be just.
Falsehood cannot become an excuse because glory still appears.
Harm is not justified by claimed good outcomes.
Judeans and Greeks alike are under the power of misalignment.
Scripture gathers the indictment: none is righteous.
Understanding and seeking have collapsed.
All turn aside from kindness.
Speech becomes grave, deception, and venom.
The mouth fills with curse and bitterness.
The feet hurry toward bloodshed.
Ways become ruin and misery.
The way of peace is unknown.
Reverence disappears from sight.
Law stops every mouth and makes the world accountable.
Law reveals misalignment but does not manufacture innocence.
Now Divine justice manifests apart from Law yet witnessed by Law and Prophets.
Divine justice comes through Messiah-trust for all who trust, without distinction.
All fall short of Divine glory.
Grace freely makes righteous through redemption in Messiah.
Messiah becomes mercy-seat sign, showing Divine justice through trust and blood.
The Divine is just and makes righteous the one from Messiah-trust.
Boasting is excluded by the law of trust.
Humanity is made righteous by trust apart from works of Law.
The Divine belongs not to Judeans only but also to nations.
The one Divine makes circumcision and uncircumcision righteous through trust.
Trust does not abolish Law; it establishes its deepest witness.

contemplative_note: Romans 3 gathers the whole world into accountability so that grace can meet all without boasting. The mystical center is not shame but the collapse of superiority before Divine justice and mercy.
